rulings.Case Title: ELAINE PERCY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion to strike portions of plaintiffs¿ fifth amended complaint is granted in part and off calendar in part. Defendant¿s motion is granted with leave to amend as to the causes of action for products liability and claims regarding false representation and punitive damages. 15 days leave to amend are given to provide plaintiffs the opportunity, if they are able to do so, to state facts sufficient to plead claims for products liability, false representation punitive damages. Plaintiffs did not substantively oppose defendant¿s motion. Defendant¿s motion is off calendar as moot as to the cause of action for Violations of Govt. Code as plaintiffs have dismissed that cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration are overruled. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Ewing¿s declaration are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MELVIN DESIN VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, June 27, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed June 15. = (503/TLJ)
